Analysis of acceptance rates by demographic groups reveals statistically meaningful disparities. Asian male applicants (Not Hispanic/Latino) show 93.1% acceptance rate with highest average loan amounts ($629,938) and income ($212,454). White male applicants (Not Hispanic/Latino) show 93.6% acceptance rate with similarly high metrics. In contrast, American Indian/Alaska Native males (Hispanic/Latino) show 83.1% acceptance rate, and Black/African American males (Not Hispanic/Latino) show 85.9% acceptance rate—approximately 8-10 percentage points lower. Black/African American females show 86.5% acceptance rate. These gaps persist despite comparable debt-to-income ratios (43-44% across groups) and loan-to-value ratios, suggesting approval decisions may correlate with race/ethnicity beyond financial metrics alone. Native Hawaiian/Pacific Islander males (Hispanic/Latino) show the lowest rate at 80.6%. The disparities are consistent across multiple demographic combinations and warrant further investigation."
}

The data shows statistically meaningful disparities in acceptance rates across racial/ethnic groups that correlate with application outcomes. Asian Male Not Hispanic (93.1% acceptance, $629,938 avg loan) and White Male Not Hispanic (93.6% acceptance, $552,232 avg loan) have notably higher acceptance rates than American Indian/Alaska Native Male Hispanic (83.1% acceptance, $439,118 avg loan) and Black/African American Male Not Hispanic (85.9% acceptance, $532,720 avg loan). However, acceptance rate disparities alone don't prove discrimination—they could reflect differences in creditworthiness metrics not fully captured here. The loan amounts and property values also vary by group. A rigorous discrimination analysis would require: (1) controlling for all underwriting factors (credit score, employment history, down payment percentage), (2) statistical testing for disparate impact, and (3) examination of whether approval denials were explained consistently across similar applicants of different races. This data alone suggests potential concerns warranting formal investigation by qualified experts."
}

Analysis reveals statistically meaningful disparities in approval rates across demographic groups that correlate with race and ethnicity. Asian Non-Hispanic and White Non-Hispanic male applicants show acceptance rates of 93.1% and 93.6% respectively, while American Indian/Alaska Native males show 83.1%, and Black/African American males show 85.9%. Female applicants across most racial groups show lower acceptance rates than their male counterparts in comparable demographics.
